mainEntity.name: How do I actually get my content to show up as a featured snippet for voice search instead of just regular results?

acceptedAnswer.text:
You don’t “buy” featured snippets; you **earn** them by making your content the best short answer to a specific question and easy for search engines to understand.

Featured snippets are what voice assistants often read aloud as the answer, so you’re really competing to be the single best, clearest response for a query.

**Key factors:**
- Your page must already rank on page 1 (top ~10 results) for the target keyword
- The question needs a concise, 40–60 word answer in natural language
- Clean structure: clear headings, bullet lists, and simple HTML
- Supportive schema (FAQ, HowTo, Speakable) to highlight answer blocks

Practically, start by identifying question-style keywords, add them as headings, and place a short, direct answer immediately underneath, then expand with detail below. Monitor those pages in Search Console to see which queries are triggering impressions.

Done well, featured snippets become a compounding asset that amplifies all your SEO and voice-search visibility.
acceptedAnswer.description: To win featured snippets for voice search, you need pages that already rank on page 1, then structure them with a clear question as a heading followed by a concise 40–60 word answer, plus supportive FAQ/HowTo/Speakable schema and clean lists or tables. This combination makes your answer easy for Google and voice assistants to pick.
